description:
You are a localization engineer who has just received an outdated CSV that contains six-language translations of user-interface messages.  
The columns inside the file are currently ordered like this:

1. key – unique identifier of the string  
2. en  – English text  
3. de  – German text  
4. fr  – French text  
5. es  – Spanish text  
6. it  – Italian text  

Your goal is to re-order the columns so that all romance-language translations sit together.  
The desired, NEW column order is:

1. key  
2. en  
3. fr  
4. es  
5. de  
6. it  

You must accomplish the task only with standard command-line utilities (e.g. cut, paste, awk, sed, etc.; no spreadsheet programs).  
After you finish, the system has to match *exactly* the file layout and log file described below.

Initial data location (already present on disk):

/home/user/project/translations/messages.csv

Expected NEW artefacts you must create:

1. /home/user/project/translations/updated_messages.csv  
   • File must contain exactly 11 lines: one header row plus ten data rows.  
   • The header row **must** be:  
     key,en,fr,es,de,it  
   • The data rows must appear in the same order they were in the original file, but with their columns re-arranged to match the new header.  
   • No extra whitespace is allowed anywhere in the file (neither before nor after commas, nor at line ends).  
   • Every line must end with a single newline character LF (`\n`).  
   • The automated test will perform a byte-for-byte comparison, so make sure you preserve all accents (e.g. “Sí”, “Félicitations”, etc.) exactly as shown in the original.

2. /home/user/project/translations/update_log.txt  
   • Plain-text file, exactly one line, case-sensitive, ending with a single LF.  
   • Content must read strictly:  
     COMPLETED

Hints (do **not** print these in your terminal):  
• `cut` can separate out the needed columns, while `paste -d,` can glue them back together.  
• Remember that “key” is column 1, so to move German (currently column 3) after Spanish (column 5) you’ll want the sequence 1,2,4,5,3,6.  
• Keep the original file untouched; write the results only to the new file shown above.  
• Create the log file only after the CSV has been written successfully.

When the tester runs, it will:

1. Read /home/user/project/translations/updated_messages.csv  
2. Verify that columns appear in the new order and contain the exact strings.  
3. Confirm that /home/user/project/translations/update_log.txt contains the single word “COMPLETED”.

Carry out the steps in a normal Linux terminal session.  Show intermediate command output if useful, but remember that only the two artefacts listed will be graded.
truth:
# ----------------------- PRE-EXISTING FILE -----------------------
Path: /home/user/project/translations/messages.csv  
Contents (11 lines, each ending with LF):

key,en,de,fr,es,it
greeting,Hello,Hallo,Bonjour,Hola,Ciao
farewell,Goodbye,Auf Wiedersehen,Au revoir,Adiós,Arrivederci
thank_you,Thank you,Danke,Merci,Gracias,Grazie
yes,Yes,Ja,Oui,Sí,Sì
no,No,Nein,Non,No,Nessuno
please,Please,Bitte,S'il vous plaît,Por favor,Per favore
sorry,Sorry,Entschuldigung,Désolé,Lo siento,Mi dispiace
welcome,Welcome,Willkommen,Bienvenue,Bienvenido,Benvenuto
congrats,Congratulations,Herzlichen Glückwunsch,Félicitations,Felicitaciones,Congratulazioni
morning,Good morning,Guten Morgen,Bonjour,Buenos días,Buongiorno

# ----------------------- EXPECTED OUTPUT FILES -----------------------

(1) /home/user/project/translations/updated_messages.csv  
Exact byte content (11 lines, each ending with LF):

key,en,fr,es,de,it
greeting,Hello,Bonjour,Hola,Hallo,Ciao
farewell,Goodbye,Au revoir,Adiós,Auf Wiedersehen,Arrivederci
thank_you,Thank you,Merci,Gracias,Danke,Grazie
yes,Yes,Oui,Sí,Ja,Sì
no,No,Non,No,Nein,Nessuno
please,Please,S'il vous plaît,Por favor,Bitte,Per favore
sorry,Sorry,Désolé,Lo siento,Entschuldigung,Mi dispiace
welcome,Welcome,Bienvenue,Bienvenido,Willkommen,Benvenuto
congrats,Congratulations,Félicitations,Felicitaciones,Herzlichen Glückwunsch,Congratulazioni
morning,Good morning,Bonjour,Buenos días,Guten Morgen,Buongiorno

(2) /home/user/project/translations/update_log.txt  
Exact byte content (1 line, ending with LF):

COMPLETED

# ----------------------- PERMISSIONS -----------------------
All files reside under /home/user, which is the agent’s normal, writable location; no special permission handling is required.
